title: Jump statements should not be redundant
description: Jump statements should not be redundantAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellredundantclumsyWhy is this an issue?Jump statements, such asreturn,yield break,goto, andcontinueare used to change the normal flow of execution in a program. However, redundant jump statements can make code difficult to read and maintain.Noncompliant code examplevoid Foo() { goto A; // Noncompliant A: while (condition1) { if (condition2) { continue; // Noncompliant } else { DoTheThing(); } } return; // Noncompliant; this is a void method }Compliant solutionvoid Foo() { while (condition1) { if (!condition2) { DoTheThing(); } } }Exceptionsreturnstatements followed by a local function declaration are not considered redundant, as they help with readability.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,i
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects redundant jump statements like return, continue, break that don't affect control flow
logic.triggering_conditions: (Return at end of void method) OR (Continue as last statement in loop) OR (Unreachable code after return)
logic.reasoning: Redundant jump statements add noise without changing control flow. This rule detects: (1) return at end of void methods, (2) continue as last statement in loop body, (3) unreachable code after unconditional return. Excludes legitimate early returns, conditional jumps, and return statements before local functions which improve readability.

should_trigger: for(int i=0; i<10; i++) { Process(i); continue; }
should_not_trigger: void Method(bool c) { if(c) return; DoSomething(); }
for(int i=0; i<10; i++) { if(Skip(i)) continue; Process(i); }
